query_normalized: predicting glass transition temperature from compositional descriptors with explainable boosting machines materials science
rationale: This paper satisfies criteria (a) and (b) as it investigates the same domain (glass materials) and measures the relationship between compositional descriptors and a fundamental glass property (glass-forming ability), which is the direct physical precursor and correlate to glass transition temperature. Although it predicts GFA rather than Tg specifically and uses XGBoost instead of Explainable Boosting Machines, it provides the essential empirical baseline and feature-importance evidence regardin
